title: ribes grossularia subsp. atlanticum
intro: Ribes grossularia subsp. atlanticum, also known as the Atlantic gooseberry, is a subspecies of the gooseberry plant native to the western coast of Europe and the northwestern coast of Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and to treat urinary tract infections, due to its high content of tartaric acid and other organic acids. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, acting as an antioxidant, and potentially helping to manage blood sugar levels. Ribes grossularia subsp. atlanticum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its berries can be eaten raw or used in jams and preserves, although caution should be exercised due to potential interactions with medications.
